# UC-14.2.23 · ICS Change Management Compliance

## Description

Logic downloads, firmware pushes, or HMI project changes without a linked work order break IEC 62443 SR-7 / internal change policy.

## Value

Logic downloads, firmware pushes, or HMI project changes without a linked work order break IEC 62443 SR-7 / internal change policy.

## Implementation

Require pre-approved WO for all downloads; correlate with maintenance windows from MES.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: PLC programming tools audit, engineering station logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="plc:download"`, `sourcetype="tia:audit"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Require pre-approved WO for all downloads; correlate with maintenance windows from MES.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="plc:download"
| lookup cmms_work_orders change_id OUTPUT wo_status requester
| where isnull(wo_status) OR wo_status!="approved"
| stats count by plc_name, engineer_id, project_version
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**ICS Change Management Compliance** — Logic downloads, firmware pushes, or HMI project changes without a linked work order break IEC 62443 SR-7 / internal change policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="plc:download"`, `sourcetype="tia:audit"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): PLC programming tools audit, engineering station logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: plc:download. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="plc:download". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(wo_status) OR wo_status!="approved"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by plc_name, engineer_id, project_version** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unauthorized downloads), Bar chart (by line), Gantt (WO vs. event time).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="plc:download"
| lookup cmms_work_orders change_id OUTPUT wo_status requester
| where isnull(wo_status) OR wo_status!="approved"
| stats count by plc_name, engineer_id, project_version
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (unauthorized downloads), Bar chart (by line), Gantt (WO vs. event time).
